evidence.organizational_memory: References specific internal systems (promptfoo.skill.*, skill-used assertions, feature/codex-skill-evals branch) and decisions about skill eval automation that are team-specific
evidence.uncertainty: Minimal—author presents decisions as finalized. Some implicit assumptions about how downstream automation will use the signals, though not framed as questions.
evidence.causal_reasoning: The parent PR adds native skill-used support, but there were still two gaps... When cli_env was set without inherit_process_env, Codex could lose basic shell tools from PATH. If a command references SKILL.md but exits 127, that is useful debugging information, but it should not be treated the same as a confirmed successful skill read.

title: fix: Preserve delimiting colons in Ruby-namespaced method names in `file://` references
author: willlockwood

evidence.lived_experience: When writing external-file-based assertions in Ruby, `fileRef.split(":")` breaks assertions using Ruby namespaced methods... so only "MyModule" gets captured as the function name, and everything else gets discarded.
evidence.uncertainty: maybe other file types too, I dunno
evidence.causal_reasoning: The problem is that split(":") on a string like `file://script.rb:MyModule::Nested.method` produces ["MyModule", "", "Nested.method"] instead of capturing the full method reference. The fix addresses this by splitting on the FIRST `:` only, which preserves the `::` Ruby namespace operator.

title: fix(app): wrap DataTable body cells in overflow-hidden container
author: MrFlounder

evidence.lived_experience: Reported by a customer after the MUI DataGrid → TanStack DataTable migration. Names longer than the column width (250px) were completely invisible. Root cause: the `<button>` was inline-block, grew to ~360px (text width)...
evidence.organizational_memory: Header cells already use this pattern (line 378-386), body cells did not. Per the migration context and existing patterns in codebase.
evidence.uncertainty: None detected. Author is confident in diagnosis and solution.
evidence.causal_reasoning: `overflow: hidden` on `display: table-cell` is not reliably defined by the CSS spec — it only formally applies to block/flex/grid containers. This caused inline-block children (buttons, links) with `truncate` to overflow fixed-width cells and become invisible in production. The wrapper `<div>` is a block element (reliable `overflow: hidden`) that constrains to the `<td>` width...
